taxonomy.label: AI compute
taxonomy.description: GPU/accelerator chips, AI servers, foundries, HBM/storage media for AI workloads, optical interconnects.

taxonomy.label: AI infrastructure - power
taxonomy.description: Power generation, fuel cells, nuclear, gas peakers, nat-gas E&P, generators, and grid hardware aimed at AI data centers.

taxonomy.label: AI infrastructure - cooling
taxonomy.description: Cooling systems, datacenter HVAC, immersion cooling.

taxonomy.label: AI infrastructure - datacenter
taxonomy.description: Datacenter REITs, networking, hardware, colocation/hosting infrastructure aimed at AI tenants.

taxonomy.label: Bitcoin mining
taxonomy.description: Companies whose primary business is bitcoin/crypto mining (often dual-classified with ai-infra-power because of GPU/HPC pivot).

taxonomy.label: Hyperscalers
taxonomy.description: Big-tech cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and the public companies that operate them.

taxonomy.label: AI applications
taxonomy.description: Software/services that consume AI.

taxonomy.label: Photonics
taxonomy.description: Optical interconnects, transceivers, silicon photonics, lasers — components specific to high-bandwidth data center networking.

taxonomy.label: HBM memory
taxonomy.description: High-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips and the DRAM makers producing them — a known supply bottleneck for AI accelerators.

taxonomy.label: AI foundries
taxonomy.description: Pure-play foundries fabricating leading-edge AI chips (e.g., TSMC).

taxonomy.label: GPU / accelerator
taxonomy.description: The GPU and custom-accelerator vendors themselves (NVIDIA, AMD, etc.) as opposed to suppliers further up the stack.

taxonomy.label: Nuclear power
taxonomy.description: Existing fission generation and the next-gen nuclear plays specifically positioned for AI-load growth.

taxonomy.label: Gas peakers / nat-gas E&P
taxonomy.description: Gas-fired peaker plants and the natural gas exploration & production companies feeding them.

taxonomy.label: Grid hardware
taxonomy.description: Transformers, switchgear, transmission equipment that lets new AI loads connect to the grid.

taxonomy.label: Datacenter REITs
taxonomy.description: Public datacenter REITs (Equinix, Digital Realty, etc.) as opposed to networking gear or colocation services.

taxonomy.label: Fuel cells
taxonomy.description: Fuel-cell power systems and adjacent distributed power equipment for datacenter-scale load growth.

taxonomy.label: Power equipment
taxonomy.description: Generators, boilers, engines, and power-system equipment that support new AI power demand.

taxonomy.label: Oilfield power services
taxonomy.description: Oilfield and energy-service companies exposed to gas supply, pressure pumping, or distributed power infrastructure.

taxonomy.label: Accelerated cloud
taxonomy.description: GPU cloud and accelerated-compute platforms serving AI training and inference workloads.

taxonomy.label: AI datacenter hosting
taxonomy.description: Colocation, hosting, and infrastructure operators pivoting capacity toward AI/HPC tenants.

taxonomy.label: Bitcoin / HPC miners
taxonomy.description: Bitcoin miners whose power and site portfolios can also support HPC or AI hosting.

taxonomy.label: Storage media
taxonomy.description: Hard-drive, NAND, and storage-media suppliers tied to AI data retention and datacenter buildouts.

taxonomy.label: Platform silicon
taxonomy.description: Broad compute silicon platforms, including CPU and accelerator-adjacent suppliers.

taxonomy.label: AI networking silicon
taxonomy.description: Custom ASIC, switching, and networking silicon suppliers for scale-out AI clusters.

taxonomy.label: Specialty foundry
taxonomy.description: Specialty semiconductor foundries and analog/mixed-signal fabs adjacent to AI hardware supply chains.
